text: Test-driven development, or TDD, is a popular approach where you write tests before writing the actual code. This method helps you focus on what your code needs to do from the start. Two important types of tests are unit tests and integration tests. Unit tests check small parts of your code, like individual functions, to make sure each works correctly on its own. Integration tests, on the other hand, check how different parts of your software work together. By using both kinds of tests, you can catch bugs early and build confidence in your code. With a strong test suite, you can make changes knowing that errors will be quickly detected.
